commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed for live Vertex proof and a small endpoint-policy correction; automation should not replace the contributor's real-environment proof.; Needs attention: The patch touches host classification and currently broadens Vertex recognition to every .rep.googleapis.com host without updating the manifest policy surface.; Review finding: [P2] Keep Vertex rep hosts in endpoin... commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Google Vertex host construction for `eu` and `us` to `aiplatform.{location}.rep.googleapis.com`, broadens Vertex hostname recognition, and adds focused host-resolution tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +63. Total +72 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main only special-cases `global`, so `eu` and `us` fall through to regional-prefix hosts. I did not perform a live Google Vertex call with credentials.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed for live Vertex proof and a small endpoint-policy correction; automation should not replace the contributor's real-environment proof.

target.title: fix(vertex): route eu/us multi-region to .rep.googleapis.com host
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89891.

Vertex AI multi-region locations (`eu`, `us`) are unreachable. `resolveGoogleVertexBaseOrigin` only special-cases `global`, so `eu`/`us` fall through to the regional-prefix form `https://${location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com` (e.g. `eu-aiplatform.googleapis.com`), which Google answers with an HTML 404. Setting `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=eu` (required for models released to the EU multi-region first, such as `gemini-3.5-flash`) breaks every model invocation.

## Changes

Following the endpoint contract in the authoritative `@google/genai` SDK (`MULTI_REGIONAL_LOCATIONS = new Set(['us', 'eu'])`, routed to `aiplatform.${location}.rep.googleapis.com`):

1. `resolveGoogleVertexBaseOrigin` (`extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`): adds a multi-region branch so `eu`/`us` resolve to `https://aiplatform.${location}.rep.googleapis.com`, keeping `global` on the unprefixed host and all regional locations on the existing prefixed form. The request path keeps `locations/${location}` unchanged, matching the working curl in the report.
2. `isGoogleVertexHostname` (`extensions/google/provider-policy.ts`): recognizes the `.rep.googleapis.com` multi-region host as a Vertex host. Without this, the new host is not classified as Vertex and provider/payload policy is bypassed for multi-region requests.

The fix uses the dedicated `.rep.googleapis.com` hosts rather than the unprefixed global host for `eu`/`us`, matching `@google/genai` and Google's current endpoint documentation. The unprefixed host would also make `eu`/`us` indistinguishable from `global` on the reverse baseUrl-to-region path.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Vertex AI `eu`/`us` multi-region invocations fail with an HTML 404 because the host is built with a regional prefix (`eu-aiplatform.googleapis.com`) instead of the multi-region host (`aiplatform.eu.rep.googleapis.com`). #89891.

**Real environment tested**: Local source checkout, Node v24, exercising the exported `resolveGoogleVertexBaseOrigin` and `isGoogleVertexHostname` directly (the same functions the Vertex transport uses to build request URLs and classify hosts), run on main and on this branch.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**: Ran the focused vitest suite that drives the real host-construction and host-recognition functions:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/vertex-multi-region-host.test.ts

**Evidence after fix**: All six cases pass against the patched code:
-- AFTER (this branch)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  6 passed (6)
eu           -> https://aiplatform.eu.rep.googleapis.com
us           -> https://aiplatform.us.rep.googleapis.com
global       -> https://aiplatform.googleapis.com
europe-west1 -> https://europe-west1-aiplatform.googleapis.com
isGoogleVertexHostname("aiplatform.eu.rep.googleapis.com") -> true

-- BEFORE (main, multi-region branch removed)
Tests  2 failed | 4 passed (6)
eu -> https://eu-aiplatform.googleapis.com   (HTML 404)
us -> https://us-aiplatform.googleapis.com   (HTML 404)

**Observed result after fix**: `eu`/`us` resolve to the `.rep.googleapis.com` multi-region hosts and are recognized as Vertex hosts; `global` and regional locations are unchanged (regression cases pass).

**Verification**: `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean; oxlint clean on the touched files.

## Note on #89901

This overlaps with #89901. The key difference is the `eu`/`us` host: the authoritative `@google/genai` SDK routes multi-region `eu`/`us` to `aiplatform.${location}.rep.googleapis.com`, not the unprefixed `aiplatform.googleapis.com`. This PR follows that contract, adds the `isGoogleVertexHostname` recognition the new host requires, and includes regression tests for `global`/`eu`/`us`/regional. Happy to consolidate if the maintainers prefer a single PR.
target.author: alkor2000
